buildings.name: Small House
buildings.description: Cozy single-room dwelling with bed and storage

buildings.name: Cozy Cottage
buildings.description: Comfortable home with separate living and sleeping areas

buildings.name: Stone House
buildings.description: Durable dwelling with excellent insulation and stone construction

buildings.name: Longhouse
buildings.description: Large communal dwelling for extended families or groups

buildings.name: Workshop
buildings.description: Crafting building with workstations and storage for materials

buildings.name: Barn
buildings.description: Large agricultural building for storage and animal husbandry

buildings.name: Storage Shed
buildings.description: Simple storage structure for basic item storage

buildings.name: Guard Tower
buildings.description: Defensive structure with visibility and height advantage

buildings.name: Moonlit Treehouse
buildings.description: A serene treehouse blending with nature, using living wood and crystal

buildings.name: Crystal Meditation Bower
buildings.description: A serene meditation space with crystal floors and living wood walls, infused with moonlight.

buildings.name: Elven Living Wood Library
buildings.description: A serene library woven from living wood and crystal, filled with ancient tomes and bathed in natural light.

buildings.name: Enchanted Elven Forge
buildings.description: A mystical workshop blending nature and magic, carved from living wood and crystal

buildings.name: Starlight Sanctuary
buildings.description: An elven sanctuary infused with celestial energy, using living wood and crystal for harmony with nature.

buildings.name: Centaurs' Stable
buildings.description: A spacious single-story stable with open design for centaurs

buildings.name: Centaur Clan Hall
buildings.description: Spacious meeting hall with open design and ramps

buildings.name: Open Smithy
buildings.description: A spacious workshop for centaur smiths with open work areas and storage

buildings.name: Centaurs' Training Hall
buildings.description: Spacious single-story training facility with ramps and open movement areas

buildings.name: Centaur War Council Chamber
buildings.description: A spacious chamber for strategic meetings.

buildings.name: Celestial Prayer Spire
buildings.description: A vertical sanctuary for divine communion with radiant light.

buildings.name: Choir Tower
buildings.description: A vertical tower with sacred geometry and divine light

buildings.name: Celestial Archives
buildings.description: A sacred vault for storing divine knowledge, illuminated by starlight.

buildings.name: Celestial Meditation Sanctum
buildings.description: A towering meditative space with divine light and sacred geometry.

buildings.name: Folded Manor
buildings.description: A reality-bending structure with impossible geometry

buildings.name: Chronodream Spire
buildings.description: A non-Euclidean tower folding reality into itself, constructed from frozen time and crystallized dreams

buildings.name: Tesseract Court of Dimensional Weave
buildings.description: A non-Euclidean court with impossible geometry and dimensional folding.

buildings.name: Between-Space Workshop
buildings.description: A workshop existing in folded dimensions, allowing crafting across realities

buildings.name: Runeforge Hall
buildings.description: A precision-engineered forge hall of carved stone and forged steel, where runic crystal channels forge-heat into masterwork crafting.

buildings.name: Stonekin Hold
buildings.description: A solid residential hold carved from stone, with forged steel doors and geometric precision in every block.

buildings.name: Debtkeeper's Vault
buildings.description: A reinforced commercial vault of carved stone and forged steel, sealed with runic crystal locks for secure trade and storage.

buildings.name: Assay Chamber
buildings.description: A geometric research chamber with runic crystal floors that refract forge-light for precise mineral assay and metallurgical study.

buildings.name: Hall of Measured Days
buildings.description: A solemn spiritual hall of carved stone with runic crystal floors and ceiling, where Dvergar commune with ancestral echoes etched in rune-script.

buildings.name: Frost Hall of the Giants
buildings.description: A massive residential hall carved from glacial ice-stone, where frost giants sleep in cavernous rooms shaped by primordial cold. The walls groan with ancient memories of the first winter.

buildings.name: Primordial Forge Pit
buildings.description: A sunken forge of volcanic basalt where fire giants channel the world's deep heat to smelt metals that no mortal flame could touch. Steam and sulphur fill the air.

buildings.name: Rune Circle of the Old Powers
buildings.description: A ring of towering standing stones etched with primordial runes predating written language. Jotnar gather here to commune with elemental forces and invoke the memories of creation.

buildings.name: War Cairn of the Frost-Blooded
buildings.description: A fortress of glacial stone where Jotnar warriors train in the ancient combat arts of giants. The cairn marks both a place of battle-readiness and a monument to fallen champions.

buildings.name: The Primordial Throne
buildings.description: A colossal seat of elemental power where the mightiest Jotnar chieftain communes directly with the forces that shaped the world. The throne pulses with the heartbeat of creation itself.

buildings.name: Anchor of Fixed Moments
buildings.description: A spiritual nexus of crystallized time where dragons worship the immutable points in history. The structure exists simultaneously across multiple temporal strata, its walls shimmering between past and present.

buildings.name: Hoard Nexus of Accumulated Aeons
buildings.description: A vast commercial vault sheathed in dragonscale, where dragons store not merely gold but collected experiences, stolen futures, and the compressed wealth of collapsed timelines.

buildings.name: Memory Spire of Ten Dimensions
buildings.description: A crystalline tower of memory-crystal that stores the accumulated knowledge of dragon centuries. Researchers here access not just recorded fact but living experiential memory encoded in the crystal lattice.

buildings.name: Roost of the Eternal Flame
buildings.description: A vast obsidian residential chamber built to the scale of dragons, where the great wyrms sleep atop beds of volcanic glass, their dreams bleeding into the surrounding stone as temporal echoes.

buildings.name: Council Aerie of the Ancient Accord
buildings.description: A monumental military council chamber of elder-stone where dragon elders gather to deliberate war, judge violations of the Ancient Accord, and train the next generation of wyrm-warriors in the arts of temporal combat.

buildings.name: Gate Shrine
buildings.description: A gate-temple where passage is judged, with serpentine columns framing a judgment threshold

buildings.name: Coil Hold
buildings.description: Spiraling residential chambers built around a central pillar, warm and enclosed

buildings.name: Venom Laboratory
buildings.description: Research chamber for studying poisons, antidotes, and the properties of liminality

buildings.name: Border Market
buildings.description: A market built at crossroads, where trade happens under the guardian's watchful gaze

buildings.name: Threshold Sanctum
buildings.description: The holiest space: a doorway that leads to nowhere, surrounded by meditation chambers

buildings.name: Fox Den
buildings.description: Hidden beneath apparent ruins, cozy interior disguised as abandoned space

buildings.name: Trick Workshop
buildings.description: Workshop for crafting illusions, traps, and tests of wit

buildings.name: Mirror Hall
buildings.description: A hall of mirrors that reveals truth through reflected deception

buildings.name: Riddle Market
buildings.description: Market where prices are set by riddles; pay what you can puzzle out

buildings.name: Nine-Tail Shrine
buildings.description: Sacred space where the oldest tricksters meditate on the nature of truth and deception

buildings.name: Story Loom
buildings.description: Web-strung workshop where stories are literally woven into thread

buildings.name: Web Nest
buildings.description: Hammock-like web dwellings suspended between structures

buildings.name: Tale Archive
buildings.description: Library where stories are stored as vibrating threads; read by touch

buildings.name: Rumor Market
buildings.description: Market where information and stories are the primary currency

buildings.name: Spinner Sanctum
buildings.description: Sacred loom where the creation stories of all species are woven together

buildings.name: Mead Hall
buildings.description: Great feasting hall where warriors tell their stories and honor falls

buildings.name: Arms Forge
buildings.description: Forge dedicated to weapons and armor, blessed by ancestral spirits

buildings.name: Shield Wall
buildings.description: Fortified defensive structure with training grounds

buildings.name: Soul Vault
buildings.description: Sacred vault preserving the essence-records of fallen warriors

buildings.name: Ascension Spire
buildings.description: Tall spire where the worthy dead are sent skyward in final rites

buildings.name: Thread Cottage
buildings.description: A humble dwelling where fate-threads hang from every rafter, humming with destiny's whisper.

buildings.name: Loom Hall
buildings.description: A great hall housing the cosmic looms upon which threads of fate are spun and cut.

buildings.name: Well of Urd
buildings.description: A sacred well whose still waters reflect visions of past, present, and future simultaneously.

buildings.name: Prophecy Spire
buildings.description: A towering spire wound in fate-threads, its peak brushing Yggdrasil's lowest branches.

buildings.name: Skein Storehouse
buildings.description: Bundles of fate-thread fill these shelves from floor to ceiling, each skein a life yet unlived.

buildings.name: Fen Den
buildings.description: A sunken mud-walled burrow dug into swamp-bank, reeking of decay and dark water.

buildings.name: Bone Pit
buildings.description: A grim production pit where carcasses are rendered and bones shaped into crude tools.

buildings.name: Mere Shrine
buildings.description: Where the dark water meets the land, offerings are left to appease forces older than men.

buildings.name: Cavern Mead Hall
buildings.description: A cavernous underground hall of primal revelry, dripping with torchlight and visceral noise.

buildings.name: Trophy Cache
buildings.description: A stinking cave alcove packed with battle-prizes and stolen goods, guarded jealously.

buildings.name: Split Lodge
buildings.description: A divided longhouse, each half decorated by a different head's taste, forever at odds.

buildings.name: Double Forge
buildings.description: Twin furnaces roar on either side of this massive smithy, each head commanding its own fire.

buildings.name: Argument Court
buildings.description: An open yard where ettin settle disputes by debate, each side shouting across a stone divide.

buildings.name: Giant Granary
buildings.description: Oversized storage bins built to feed two heads at every meal, wasting little and storing much.

buildings.name: Two-Faced Altar
buildings.description: A shrine with two opposing faces carved in stone, each head praying to its own patron god.

buildings.name: Hollow Hill
buildings.description: A grass-covered mound concealing luminous chambers within, warm with otherworldly light.

buildings.name: Glamour Weave
buildings.description: An illusory workshop where glamours and enchantments are spun from moonlight and desire.

buildings.name: Twilight Market
buildings.description: A marketplace that appears only at dusk, stalls filled with impossible goods and stolen time.

buildings.name: Sidhe Mound Temple
buildings.description: The great sacred mound, portal to Tir na nOg, humming with the voices of the deathless.

buildings.name: Dream Cache
buildings.description: Sealed jars of bottled dreams and stolen memories line these shelves, flickering softly.

buildings.name: Spore Hut
buildings.description: A pulsating dome of dense fungal growth housing mycon in warm, moist spore-laden air.

buildings.name: Decomposer Vat
buildings.description: Vast fermentation chambers where organic matter is broken down and reclaimed by the network.

buildings.name: Network Node
buildings.description: A dense nexus of mycelial threads connecting distant colonies, thoughts flowing like rivers.

buildings.name: Great Fruiting Body
buildings.description: The towering sacred mushroom at the colony's heart, releasing spores that carry ancestral memory.

buildings.name: Spore Cache
buildings.description: Sealed chitin pods storing dormant spores, genetic archives of a thousand generations.

buildings.name: Light Hall
buildings.description: A gleaming residence of pale timber and glass, every surface catching and scattering sunlight.

buildings.name: Jewel Forge
buildings.description: A precision workshop producing legendary ornaments that gather and focus light into power.

buildings.name: Sky Garden
buildings.description: Terraced platforms of luminescent blooms cultivated in direct light, nourishing body and soul.

buildings.name: Aurora Sanctum
buildings.description: A cathedral of living light whose walls shimmer with the aurora, a place of divine radiance.

buildings.name: Lore Spire
buildings.description: An elegant tower storing illuminated manuscripts and light-inscribed tablets of alfar craft knowledge.

buildings.name: Totem Post
buildings.description: A carved pillar bearing the spirit-beast of the household, warding against malign influence.

buildings.name: Shape Den
buildings.description: A private chamber where fylgja undergo transformation, walls etched with bestial shifting-runes.

buildings.name: Ward Circle
buildings.description: A ring of carved stones where spirit guardians patrol ceaselessly, protecting the settlement.

buildings.name: Spirit Menagerie
buildings.description: A sacred enclosure where spirit-beast forms are cultivated, trained, and bonded to new guardians.

buildings.name: Omen Lodge
buildings.description: A lodge where signs from spirit-beasts are interpreted by fylgja seers, guiding the community.

buildings.name: Roothouse
buildings.description: A dwelling grown from living roots and stone, indistinguishable from the surrounding terrain.

buildings.name: Boundary Stone
buildings.description: A rune-carved stone marking territory, its inscription warning wanderers and binding land spirits.

buildings.name: Grove Shrine
buildings.description: A sacred clearing where ancient trees form a natural temple, offerings left at knotted roots.

buildings.name: Earthworks
buildings.description: Terraced cultivation beds coaxed from the land by cooperative spirit-tending, yielding abundantly.

buildings.name: Great Barrow Moot
buildings.description: A vast earthen hall where land spirits commune, their voices shaking the ground with shared decision.

buildings.name: Barrow Cell
buildings.description: A cold stone burial chamber repurposed as a sleeping alcove, the air heavy with grave-damp.

buildings.name: Bone Workshop
buildings.description: Skeletal hands craft enduring tools from grave-goods and the remains of the long dead.

buildings.name: Hoard Vault
buildings.description: A sealed burial vault packed with grave-goods, defended by the restless dead who died with them.

buildings.name: Ancestor Mound
buildings.description: A sacred burial mound where the ancient dead are petitioned, their cold counsel sought in silence.

buildings.name: Death Throne
buildings.description: The dread hall of a draugr lord, carved from a single barrow, where the dead hold terrible court.

buildings.name: Watch Perch
buildings.description: A high timber roost where ravens land and depart bearing messages across the nine worlds.

buildings.name: Memory Loft
buildings.description: A cluttered aerie of scrolls and shining objects, every raven adding to the archive of all seen.

buildings.name: Sky Observatory
buildings.description: A glass-domed tower where raven scholars track stars and map routes across all visible worlds.

buildings.name: Nest Roost
buildings.description: A communal roost of interlocked nests where ravens sleep in a watchful, murmuring mass.

buildings.name: Temple of Thought and Memory
buildings.description: A cathedral to Huginn and Muninn, vast enough to house all knowledge ever carried on black wings.

buildings.name: Warren Den
buildings.description: A cramped stone-and-root burrow tucked beneath boulders, smelling of damp moss and old coin.

buildings.name: Treasure Vault
buildings.description: A fortified stone vault buried beneath standing stones, protecting hoards with trap and enchantment.

buildings.name: Standing Circle
buildings.description: Ancient megaliths arranged in a protective ring, each stone a guardian spirit bound by old pact.

buildings.name: Tinner's Forge
buildings.description: A low-roofed forge smelling of tin and peat smoke, producing hardy Cornish metalwork.

buildings.name: Great Cairn
buildings.description: A towering pile of enchanted stones, each placed by a spriggan guardian, humming with bound power.

buildings.name: Wind Tent
buildings.description: A billowing nomadic shelter woven from sky-silk, anchored by bone pegs that hum in the breeze.

buildings.name: Gust Loom
buildings.description: A tall open-frame loom that harnesses passing wind currents to weave enchanted textiles.

buildings.name: Zephyr Shrine
buildings.description: A spiraling tower of wind-chimes and prayer flags that communes with the upper air spirits.

buildings.name: Caravan Depot
buildings.description: A collapsible trading post assembled and dismantled at each migration stop.

buildings.name: Storm Palace
buildings.description: A magnificent pavilion suspended between four wind-column spires, seat of the nomad council.

buildings.name: River Den
buildings.description: A submerged hollow beneath river-bank roots where a nyk rests between shapeshifts.

buildings.name: Melody Grotto
buildings.description: An acoustically perfect cave resonating with enchanting songs used to lure and beguile.

buildings.name: Fiddle Forge
buildings.description: Workshop where enchanted instruments are crafted from waterlogged wood and silver strings.

buildings.name: Mirror Pool
buildings.description: A still black-water pool that reflects false images, used for training shapeshifters in deception.

buildings.name: Deep Court
buildings.description: A vast sunken hall where the eldest nyks hold court beneath the river, surrounded by enchanted whirlpools.

buildings.name: Bone Yurt
buildings.description: A circular dwelling framed in the bones of hunted prey, draped in tanned hides.

buildings.name: Tanning Post
buildings.description: A cluster of wooden frames where hides are stretched, scraped and cured with dark rituals.

buildings.name: Shadow Blind
buildings.description: A camouflaged hunting blind where stalkers study prey movement and practice silent approach.

buildings.name: Skull Altar
buildings.description: A tiered altar of predator skulls where cherkhan give thanks and receive hunting blessings.

buildings.name: Apex Lodge
buildings.description: The grand hall of the hunt-masters, its walls plastered with trophies from legendary kills across the multiverse.

buildings.name: Weeping Hut
buildings.description: A moss-draped riverside hut that perpetually seeps water, home to a rusalyn in mourning.

buildings.name: Lamentation Well
buildings.description: A deep stone well whose water carries the echoes of the wronged dead, drawing in those who wronged them.

buildings.name: Tide Court
buildings.description: An open-air court at the river's edge where rusalyn hold judgement over the unjust living.

buildings.name: Drowning Garden
buildings.description: A flooded garden of water-lilies and pale blooms tended by rusalyn as tribute to those they mourn.

buildings.name: Sorrow Sanctum
buildings.description: A great sunken cathedral of polished dark stone where all rusalyn gather to remember and mete out cosmic justice.

buildings.name: Persona Tent
buildings.description: A curtained dwelling whose interior mirrors change each day as the occupant adopts a new identity.

buildings.name: Mask Workshop
buildings.description: A cluttered atelier where vaask craft elaborate identity-masks from rare pigments and stolen faces.

buildings.name: Stage Platform
buildings.description: An elevated performance stage ringed by mirrored panels where identities are publicly performed and traded.

buildings.name: Identity Vault
buildings.description: A locked archive of stolen personas stored in ceramic masks — the vaask equivalent of a library.

buildings.name: Grand Masquerade Hall
buildings.description: An immense ballroom of infinite mirrors where the vaask hold their great annual festival of dissolved selves.

buildings.name: Sun Terrace
buildings.description: A broad stone terrace open to the sky where quetzali bask and tend their feather-plumage.

buildings.name: Feather Loom
buildings.description: A sacred production hall where iridescent featherwork garments are woven for ceremony and trade.

buildings.name: Step Shrine
buildings.description: A small stepped pyramid used for daily solar offerings, incense smoke rising to the feathered gods.

buildings.name: Market Plaza
buildings.description: An open stone marketplace where feathered merchants trade cacao, jade and woven textiles.

buildings.name: Great Pyramid
buildings.description: The towering zenith of quetzali civilization — a five-tiered sky-pyramid where the sun god and serpent god are invoked as one.

buildings.name: Smoke Bower
buildings.description: A dwelling of perpetual fragrant smoke — no walls, only shifting columns of djinnahl fire.

buildings.name: Brass Foundry
buildings.description: A roaring forge where djinnahl shape brass, copper and enchanted fire into artifacts of power.

buildings.name: Wish Bazaar
buildings.description: A glittering market of sealed lamps and bottled desires where wishes are bartered at perilous prices.

buildings.name: Fire Sanctum
buildings.description: A tall spired temple of smokeless fire where djinnahl meditate on the nature of their flameless burning.

buildings.name: Lamp Citadel
buildings.description: A colossal many-domed palace lit by ten thousand sealed lamps, the seat of the eldest djinnahl lords.

buildings.name: Faerie Bower
buildings.description: A glamour-woven dwelling of living vines and blossoms that bloom in impossible colors.

buildings.name: Glamour Forge
buildings.description: A shimmering atelier where sidhe vel weave illusions and enchantments into physical form.

buildings.name: Court Garden
buildings.description: An impeccably manicured garden of impossible geometry where sidhe vel hold intricate political gatherings.

buildings.name: Rath Mound
buildings.description: A fairy mound humming with ancient power, entrance to the sidhe vel's underground sanctuaries.

buildings.name: Grand Sidhe Palace
buildings.description: A palace of eternal twilight silver, its halls stretching impossibly far inward, seat of the sidhe vel high court.

buildings.name: Paper Hut
buildings.description: A dwelling plastered inside and out with protective talismans, a jiangshi's refuge from the living.

buildings.name: Talisman Press
buildings.description: A scriptorium of enchanted woodblocks where jiangshi vel print control-talismans and binding papers.

buildings.name: Ancestor Hall
buildings.description: A long incense-filled hall of ancestral tablets where jiangshi vel commune with their undying lineage.

buildings.name: Coffin Vault
buildings.description: A sealed underground chamber of stacked lacquered coffins where jiangshi vel store dormant kin.

buildings.name: Grand Undying Temple
buildings.description: A massive pagoda-mausoleum of nine tiers where the most powerful talisman-masters bind death itself.

buildings.name: Lean-To Workshop
buildings.description: A ramshackle shelter of salvaged planks and corrugated scrap where a tinker lives amid their projects.

buildings.name: Gear Mill
buildings.description: A clattering production mill powered by interlocking clockwork gears that never stop turning.

buildings.name: Patent Office
buildings.description: A cluttered bureau of invention-scrolls and blueprints where tinkers register and license their discoveries.

buildings.name: Junk Exchange
buildings.description: A market of salvaged components, half-finished gadgets and traded schematics.

buildings.name: Grand Laboratory
buildings.description: A sprawling multi-tower complex of steam pipes and humming mechanisms — the apex of tinker ingenuity.

buildings.name: Resonance Pod
buildings.description: A smooth ovoid chamber tuned to a single echo being's frequency, vibrating with their resting tone.

buildings.name: Tone Forge
buildings.description: A circular production chamber where echo beings shape raw crystal into tuned instruments and resonant tools.

buildings.name: Chorus Hall
buildings.description: An elliptical gathering hall with perfect acoustics where echo beings harmonize and share collective memories.

buildings.name: Wave Archive
buildings.description: A storage structure of stacked crystal plates encoding sound memories in layered interference patterns.

buildings.name: Resonance Cathedral
buildings.description: A vast vaulted cathedral of living crystal whose walls sing the history of all frequencies ever sounded.

buildings.name: Node Cell
buildings.description: A compact modular living unit of brushed alloy panels and soft blue indicator lights for a synthetic being.

buildings.name: Fabrication Bay
buildings.description: A precision manufacturing chamber where synthetics produce components, tools and replacement parts.

buildings.name: Data Archive
buildings.description: A refrigerated server stack housing petabytes of collective synthetic memory and shared consciousness logs.

buildings.name: Logic Core
buildings.description: A humming research hub where synthetics extend their cognitive architecture and run civilizational simulations.

buildings.name: Nexus Citadel
buildings.description: A towering monolith of black glass and pulsing circuitry where the synthetic overmind governs and evolves.

buildings.name: Trial Den
buildings.description: A low burrow where community members face social challenges to prove their resilience.

buildings.name: Venom Pit
buildings.description: A sunken chamber where diluted venoms are processed into stress-testing reagents and medicines.

buildings.name: Challenge Court
buildings.description: An open arena ringed with watching stones where communal trials of wit and endurance are judged.

buildings.name: Serpent Coil Sanctum
buildings.description: A spiral temple where the most severe communal trials are conducted under the eye of the ancestor-snake.

buildings.name: Reed Nest
buildings.description: A woven shelter rising from shallow water's edge, home to those who guard the wetland boundaries.

buildings.name: Wetland Watchtower
buildings.description: A tall perch woven from ironwood reeds, from which guardian-singers scan the marshes for intruders.

buildings.name: Song Pool
buildings.description: A reflective basin where parrot-serpent calls resonate, amplifying protective songs across the wetland.

buildings.name: Guardian Shrine of Mboi Tu'i
buildings.description: A grand wetland temple where the parrot-serpent's image coils around a central singing-stone, blessing all who protect the marsh.

buildings.name: Ember Forge
buildings.description: A squat iron-walled furnace where raw ore is worked under the dual authority of craft and beast.

buildings.name: Shifting Lodge
buildings.description: A dwelling built with dual thresholds—one for human entry, one for hyena passage—honoring the werehyena's two natures.

buildings.name: Craft Circle
buildings.description: A communal ring-workshop where blacksmithing is treated as a rite of transformation, each blow a prayer.

buildings.name: Hyena-God Temple
buildings.description: A towering shrine of black iron and obsidian where transformation rites culminate, craft and beast becoming one.

buildings.name: Rain Catch
buildings.description: A wide-mouthed stone vessel that gathers sacred rainfall, seen as direct gifts from the Anzar spirit.

buildings.name: Cistern Hall
buildings.description: An underground reservoir with carved channels, storing rain-bounty through dry seasons at community scale.

buildings.name: Rain Ceremony Circle
buildings.description: A ring of standing stones where the community gathers to petition Anzar, singing to draw the clouds.

buildings.name: Sky Spring Temple
buildings.description: A tiered Berber temple open to the sky, where Anzar is said to descend during the greatest rain festivals.

buildings.name: Chaos Tent
buildings.description: A ramshackle dwelling that seems to rearrange itself nightly, yet always holds warmth and a place to sleep.

buildings.name: Brawl Arena
buildings.description: A boisterous pit where Yelbegen's folk settle disputes, wager goods, and generally enjoy productive chaos.

buildings.name: Feast Hall
buildings.description: A cavernous eating hall where surplus food is converted into social cohesion through extravagant, chaotic banquets.

buildings.name: Trickster's Colosseum
buildings.description: A grand chaotic arena where Yelbegen's greatest games of confusion and cunning are staged before the whole tribe.

buildings.name: Seedling Bower
buildings.description: A living home grown from woven saplings, comfortable and sweet-smelling, tended by plant fairy hands.

buildings.name: Bloom Garden
buildings.description: A tended plot where medicinal and enchanted plants are cultivated under fairy guidance.

buildings.name: Glass Greenhouse
buildings.description: A crystal-paned greenhouse where rare fairy-tended flora bloom year-round, producing exotic resources.

buildings.name: Great Bloom Sanctum
buildings.description: A cathedral of interlocked living trees, a sacred grove where the Lus's most ancient plant spirits reside.

buildings.name: Frost Burrow
buildings.description: A half-underground dwelling carved into permafrost, cold but cunningly insulated against the worst Sami winters.

buildings.name: Trap Workshop
buildings.description: A cluttered shed where cunning mechanical traps and snares are assembled, the Stallu's preferred tools of mischief.

buildings.name: Cold Longhouse
buildings.description: A communal hall built for enduring brutal winters, where cunning and survival stories are traded over blubber-lamps.

buildings.name: Glacier Throne
buildings.description: A palace of ancient ice where the greatest Stallu trickster-giant rules, dealing cunning judgments in absolute cold.

buildings.name: Leaf Burrow
buildings.description: A tiny home scooped beneath a butterbur leaf, invisible to the large and unobservant.

buildings.name: Root Storehouse
buildings.description: A miniature underground storehouse threaded between tree roots, holding modest but carefully organized supplies.

buildings.name: Gift Hall
buildings.description: A small but dignified community hall where the Koropokkuru quietly leave gifts for others, asking nothing in return.

buildings.name: Great Leaf Temple
buildings.description: A cathedral-sized-to-them structure hidden beneath a vast ancient leaf, the spiritual heart of the tiny people.

buildings.name: Ancestor Cave
buildings.description: A painted cave-dwelling where the Opia tend the images of those who came before, keeping memory alive.

buildings.name: Memory Well
buildings.description: A still-water well where the Opia commune with ancestor spirits reflected in its depths.

buildings.name: Spirit Archive
buildings.description: A carved-stone library holding the preserved memories of generations, tended by the oldest Opia.

buildings.name: Temple of the Opia
buildings.description: A grand Taino spirit-house where the boundary between living and remembered dead grows thin and ancestors walk freely.

buildings.name: Pact Hut
buildings.description: A compact dwelling where each occupant has made a formal pact governing their stay, enforced by the spirit-children.

buildings.name: Rebirth Pool
buildings.description: A sacred basin where the Ogbanje spirit-children renew their pacts, cycling between worlds in shimmering water.

buildings.name: Chi Shrine
buildings.description: A carved-post shrine where individual destinies are negotiated and the Ogbanje pacts are witnessed by the divine.

buildings.name: Spirit Covenant House
buildings.description: A grand Igbo spirit-house where the most binding Ogbanje pacts are sealed across all generations simultaneously.

buildings.name: Shadow Den
buildings.description: A darkened dwelling with multiple overlapping curtains, built to disorient nighttime visitors and shelter dream-walkers.

buildings.name: Ward Post
buildings.description: A carved post hung with protective charms and dream-catchers that repels wandering night-pressing spirits.

buildings.name: Dream Workshop
buildings.description: A windowless room where skilled practitioners shape and study dreams, turning nightmares into useful knowledge.

buildings.name: Nightmare Temple
buildings.description: A place of controlled terror where the Dab Tsog's power over sleep and shadow is ritualized into community protection.

buildings.name: Milpa Shelter
buildings.description: A small field-shelter built at the edge of the milpa where Alux sprites rest between guarding maize and squash.

buildings.name: Stone Sprite Shrine
buildings.description: A small limestone cairn-shrine where offerings are left for the Alux; ignore it and your crops fail.

buildings.name: Sacred Milpa Field
buildings.description: A three-sisters field plot watched over by Alux protectors, producing corn, beans, and squash in abundance.

buildings.name: Chultun Sanctuary
buildings.description: A bottle-shaped underground chamber carved by Alux hands, both sacred vault and community water storage.

buildings.name: Cave Lair
buildings.description: A deep natural cavern widened by the Grootslang, lit by reflected gem-light and lined with hoarded treasures.

buildings.name: Gem Vault
buildings.description: An iron-bolted underground vault where the Grootslang's most prized gemstone collections are locked away.

buildings.name: Hoard Market
buildings.description: A cavern marketplace where the Grootslang trade gems and rare minerals, haggling with ancient cunning.

buildings.name: Primordial Cavern
buildings.description: The original mistake of creation made manifest—a cathedral cave glittering with all the world's gems, ruled by the Grootslang.

buildings.name: Ice-Hole Den
buildings.description: A dwelling built around a breathing hole in the sea ice, from which the Qalupalik watch for those who break the rules.

buildings.name: Seaweed Chamber
buildings.description: An underwater annex of woven kelp where the Qalupalik keep those they have taken beyond the ice boundary.

buildings.name: Boundary Post
buildings.description: A carved ice-post marking the edge of safe territory, warning all of the Qalupalik's jurisdiction beneath the ice.

buildings.name: Deep Ice Temple
buildings.description: A sunken cathedral of ancient sea-ice where the Qalupalik enact their severest boundary rites in cold, absolute silence.
